formal_definition: Share of a country's tertiary-educated (or otherwise high-skilled) native population residing abroad, typically measured as stock of emigrants in OECD destinations divided by stock of educated natives at home plus abroad.

Aggregate stock of education, skills, and training resident in a country's population, typically operationalized via tertiary attainment counts, occupation-specific licensure, or skill-weighted labor force.
Cross-border private transfers from migrants to households or institutions in origin countries.
Movement of emigrants back to their country of origin, with attendant transfer of human capital, financial capital, and norms acquired abroad.
Population of co-nationals residing abroad who maintain economic, social, or informational ties with the origin country, mediating trade, FDI, and knowledge flows.
Country- or region-level production of new technical knowledge, typically measured by patent applications, scientific publications, or new product introductions.
Net rate of new firm formation and labor productivity in the origin economy, sensitive to labor supply and capital reallocation following migration shocks.
Prevailing beliefs and practices regarding gender roles, female autonomy, and intra-household bargaining power.
Aggregate health outcomes of an origin-country population, typically measured by infant mortality, life expectancy, and disease prevalence.
Household-level welfare outcomes including real income, consumption expenditure, food security, and health-care access at origin.

Destination-country institutional rules — visa caps, eligibility criteria, freedom-of-movement agreements, refugee resettlement programs, lottery procedures — that determine whether and how citizens of an origin country may emigrate. Used as the source of identifying variation in causal studies of migration effects.
Share of children under working age engaged in economic labor, often used as an indicator of household financial distress.
Quality of democratic institutions and political accountability in the origin country, measured by indices of electoral integrity, civil liberties, and political participation.
